name: Discrete Manufacturing Subpack
description: Vertical intelligence subpack for Discrete Manufacturing covering Automotive/EV, aerospace, industrial equipment, electronics, medical devices, heavy machinery, robotics, and rail/marine. Adds segment-specific pains, KPIs, signal rules, personas, formulas, benchmarks, regulatory factors, tech systems, discovery questions, objections, buying triggers, and worked examples. Inherits foundational frameworks from manufacturing-master-v1.

pains.name: Warranty Claim Spike from Fastener Torque Inconsistency
pains.description: Inconsistent torque application during assembly leading to field failures, warranty claims, and potential recall risk in high-vibration environments.
symptoms: Warranty claims clustering around fastening-related failures
Torque audit pass rate <95%
Field service reports citing 'loose bolt' or 'separation'
No statistical process control on torque tools
Tool calibration drift >2% between checks

pains.name: BOM Explosion and Revision Chaos
pains.description: Engineering change orders creating BOM version mismatches between design, planning, and shop floor systems causing wrong parts procurement and assembly errors.
symptoms: BOM versions differ across ERP, PLM, and MES
Engineering change order cycle time >5 days
Wrong-part assembly incidents >1/month
Purchasing orders against obsolete revisions
No single source of BOM truth

pains.name: CNC Machine Tool Wear and Dimensional Drift
pains.description: Progressive tool wear in machining operations causing dimensional variation, increased inspection burden, and scrap before adjustment.
symptoms: CMM measurement drift trend correlating to tool life
In-process inspection rejects increasing over tool life
Tool changes based on time not condition
Surface finish degradation before nominal size violation
No tool condition monitoring system

pains.name: Assembly Line Balancing and Takt Time Misses
pains.description: Mixed-model assembly lines unable to maintain takt time due to station overload, operator variability, and sequencing complexity.
symptoms: Cycle time variation >15% station-to-station
Line stop for station overload >3x/shift
WIP accumulation at specific stations
Overtime required to meet daily build target
No dynamic line rebalancing capability

pains.name: Welding Quality Variation and Porosity
pains.description: Inconsistent weld quality in fabrication operations causing structural failures, rework, and customer rejection in safety-critical applications.
symptoms: Weld reject rate >3% on critical joints
X-ray/UT inspection findings increasing
Welder-to-welder quality variation >2 sigma
Weld parameter settings not monitored
No weld data logging or traceability

pains.name: Surface Coating and Finish Defects
pains.description: Paint, powder coat, anodize, or plating defects causing cosmetic rejects, environmental compliance issues, and customer returns.
symptoms: Coating reject rate >5% on cosmetic surfaces
Color matching complaints from customers
Overspray or drip marks requiring touch-up
Environmental permit violations related to VOCs
No real-time coating thickness monitoring

pains.name: Electronics SMT Placement Accuracy and Solder Defects
pains.description: Surface mount technology placement errors and solder joint defects causing board failures, rework, and reliability issues.
symptoms: SMT placement accuracy >50 microns off
Solder defect rate (voids, bridging) >2%
AOI false positive rate >10%
Reflow profile not monitored per board
Component traceability gaps

pains.name: GD&T and Dimensional Measurement Bottleneck
pains.description: Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) and inspection backlog creating production queue delays and delayed quality release.
symptoms: CMM queue time >20% of total lead time
First article inspection cycle >3 days
Inspection staff overtime >20%
Manual CMM programming consuming engineering time
No automated in-process metrology

pains.name: Casting and Forging Defects (Porosity, Shrinkage, Cracks)
pains.description: Foundry and forge process variation causing internal defects requiring scrap, rework, or downstream machining complications.
symptoms: Scrap rate >8% in casting operations
X-ray inspection revealing porosity clusters
Machining chatter from hard spots in castings
No real-time solidification modeling
Die/tool wear not tracked to part quality

pains.name: Kitting and Sequencing Errors in Assembly
pains.description: Incorrect component kits or wrong sequence delivery to assembly stations causing line stops, rework, and part mix-ups.
symptoms: Kitting error rate >1%
Assembly line stops for missing parts >2x/day
Wrong component installed despite barcode checks
WIP buffers growing at kitting area
No automated kit verification system

pains.name: Robotics Path Programming and Cycle Time Inefficiency
pains.description: Industrial robot paths not optimized for cycle time, causing bottlenecks and underutilization of automation investment.
symptoms: Robot cycle time >10% above simulation baseline
Teach pendant programming consuming engineering hours
Collision or near-miss incidents during operation
Path optimization only during initial commissioning
No continuous cycle time improvement program

pains.name: Additive Manufacturing Build Failure and Post-Processing
pains.description: 3D printing build failures in production parts causing material waste, delayed delivery, and inconsistent mechanical properties.
symptoms: Build failure rate >15% in production AM
Post-processing time >50% of total part time
Mechanical property variation batch-to-batch
No real-time melt pool monitoring
Qualification/certification cycle >6 months

pains.name: Sheet Metal Bend and Punch Accuracy
pains.description: Press brake and punch press operations producing parts out of tolerance due to springback, tool wear, and material variation.
symptoms: First article pass rate <90% on new bend programs
Springback compensation trial-and-error consuming setup time
Tool wear causing burr growth and edge cracks
Material thickness variation from coil to coil
No automatic bend angle correction

pains.name: EV Battery Module Assembly Contamination
pains.description: Cleanroom-level contamination control gaps in EV battery module/pack assembly causing cell degradation, thermal runaway risk, and warranty exposure.
symptoms: Particle count >Class 8 in critical assembly zones
Cell tab welding contamination causing resistance variation
Thermal interface material application inconsistency
No environmental monitoring data logging
Battery pack failure rate in early life >0.5%

pains.name: Aerospace Special Process Nadcap Audit Failures
pains.description: Heat treat, chemical processing, NDT, and welding operations failing Nadcap audits threatening supplier status with primes.
symptoms: Nadcap non-conformance findings >3 per audit
Pyrometry or chemistry out of specification
Operator certification gaps
Furnace survey overdue
Customer (Boeing, Airbus) SCARs increasing

pains.name: Medical Device Validation and V&V Backlog
pains.description: Software validation, process validation (IQ/OQ/PQ), and design verification activities creating release bottlenecks for regulated medical devices.
symptoms: V&V cycle time >6 months per product change
Validation protocols requiring >3 review cycles
FDA 483 observations on validation gaps
No automated test execution or reporting
Traceability matrix maintained manually

pains.name: Supply Chain Component Allocation Crisis
pains.description: Semiconductor and critical component allocation shortages forcing expediting, alternative sourcing, and production mix changes.
symptoms: Lead times for critical ICs >52 weeks
Multiple ECNs for component substitution
Production mix changed to prioritize available parts
Broker purchases at 3-10x standard price
No component lifecycle monitoring system

pains.name: Digital Thread Gap from CAD to Machine
pains.description: Design intent lost in translation from CAD/CAM to CNC program causing rework, scrap, and extended setup times.
symptoms: CAM programming time >40% of total setup
Design changes not propagating to shop programs
Post-processor errors causing machine crashes
No digital twin of machining process
Manual G-code edits without version control

kpis.name: Torque Process Capability (Cpk)
kpis.formula: min[(USL - Mean) / (3σ), (Mean - LSL) / (3σ)]

kpis.name: BOM Accuracy Rate
kpis.formula: (BOMs with Zero Discrepancies / Total BOMs Checked) x 100

kpis.name: CMM Utilization Rate
kpis.formula: (CMM Measurement Time / Available CMM Time) x 100

kpis.name: First Article Inspection Pass Rate
kpis.formula: (FAIs Passing on First Submission / Total FAIs Submitted) x 100

kpis.name: SMT Defects Per Million Opportunities
kpis.formula: (SMT Defects / SMT Defect Opportunities) x 1,000,000

kpis.name: Welding Reject Rate
kpis.formula: (Welds Failing Inspection / Total Welds Inspected) x 100

kpis.name: Kitting Accuracy Rate
kpis.formula: (Correct Kits / Total Kits Verified) x 100

kpis.name: Robot Cell OEE
kpis.formula: Availability x Performance x Quality (robot-specific)

kpis.name: Casting Scrap Rate
kpis.formula: (Scrap Castings / Total Castings Poured) x 100

kpis.name: Coating Thickness Process Capability (Cpk)
kpis.formula: min[(USL - Mean) / (3σ), (Mean - LSL) / (3σ)]

kpis.name: Engineering Change Order Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average days from ECO submission to approval + implementation

kpis.name: CAM Programming Time per Part
kpis.formula: Total CAM programming hours / Number of new part programs

kpis.name: Assembly Line Balance Efficiency
kpis.formula: (Sum of all task times) / (Number of stations x Cycle time of bottleneck) x 100

kpis.name: Nadcap Audit Findings Count
kpis.formula: Total non-conformance findings per Nadcap audit cycle

kpis.name: EV Battery Pack Early Life Failure Rate
kpis.formula: (Battery pack failures in first 90 days / Total packs shipped) x 100

kpis.name: Additive Manufacturing Build Success Rate
kpis.formula: (Successful builds / Total builds attempted) x 100

kpis.name: Sheet Metal First Article Pass Rate
kpis.formula: (First articles passing inspection / Total first articles) x 100

kpis.name: Tool Condition Monitoring Index
kpis.formula: Composite of spindle load, vibration RMS, acoustic emission, and flank wear measurements normalized to baseline

kpis.name: Component Traceability Coverage
kpis.formula: (Units with full component serial/lot traceability / Total units produced) x 100

kpis.name: Verification and Validation Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average days from V&V protocol approval to final report sign-off

kpis.name: Assembly Sequence Compliance Rate
kpis.formula: (Operations performed in correct sequence / Total operations verified) x 100

kpis.name: NDT Defect Detection Rate
kpis.formula: (Defects detected by NDT / Total defects found including downstream) x 100

kpis.name: Cleanroom Particulate Count Compliance
kpis.formula: (Sampling periods within class limit / Total sampling periods) x 100

kpis.name: Press Brake Setup Time
kpis.formula: Average time from last part of previous job to first good part of new job

kpis.name: Firmware Flash Success Rate
kpis.formula: (Successful firmware flashes / Total flash attempts) x 100

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Multiple torque tool calibration records past due in plant audit data
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Fastener quality control degrading; warranty risk and field failure probability increasing

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: ERP and PLM show different active BOM revisions for same part number
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Engineering change control breakdown; wrong-part assembly risk and production scrap imminent

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Machine monitoring data shows progressive spindle load increase on critical machining centers
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Tool wear or bearing degradation accelerating; dimensional drift and scrap risk rising

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Andon pull frequency increasing at specific stations over 2+ weeks
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Station-level bottleneck or quality issue emerging; takt time risk and overtime trigger

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Welding power supply data shows current/voltage outside WPS tolerance bands
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Weld quality at risk; structural integrity and NDT failure probability elevated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Reflow oven temperature profile monitoring shows zone temperatures drifting from recipe
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Solder joint quality degrading; voids, bridging, and board failure risk increasing

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Inspection planning data shows CMM queue growing >25% over baseline
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Measurement bottleneck constraining throughput; production release delays and WIP accumulation

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Radiographic inspection reveals porosity concentration in specific mold cavities or heat numbers
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Process parameter (pouring temp, cooling rate, metal treatment) out of control; scrap wave imminent

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: MES or warehouse data shows kitting error rate trending above 0.5%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Wrong-part assembly risk; line stops and rework costs escalating

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Robot controller logs show collision or torque limit events increasing
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Path programming or fixture drift degrading robot performance; cycle time and safety risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Build failures clustering on specific geometries, orientations, or material lots
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Design-for-AM gap or process parameter issue; qualification timeline and cost at risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CMM data on bent parts shows angle deviation increasing for specific material lots or bend radii
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Material properties varying or tool compensation inadequate; first article pass rate dropping

signalRules.signalName: EV Battery Pack Thermal Runaway Incident
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Field report or test data showing battery thermal event or cell-to-cell variation >spec
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Critical safety and recall risk; contamination or assembly process failure likely root cause

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PRI Nadcap audit report shows non-conformances in heat treat, chemical processing, or NDT
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Special process control failure; aerospace prime supplier status at risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: FDA inspection 483 citing computer system validation, process validation, or design verification gaps
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Medical device V&V system failure; product release and market authorization at risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Major distributor (Arrow, Avnet, DigiKey) issues allocation notice for critical components
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Semiconductor supply constraint hitting production; expediting and redesign costs incoming

signalRules.signalName: CAD Revision Not Propagated to CAM
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PLM shows CAD revision change but CNC program version remains unchanged
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Digital thread broken; obsolete geometry being machined risking scrap and customer rejection

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Automated Optical Inspection false positive rate increasing >15% over baseline
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Inspection system mis-calibrated or recipe outdated; unnecessary rework and throughput loss

signalRules.signalName: Customer SCAR (Supplier Corrective Action Request) Influx
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Multiple SCARs received from key customer (OEM or prime) in <90 days
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Systematic quality or delivery failure; contract risk and potential volume reduction

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Environmental monitoring records show particle count exceeding class limit in controlled zone
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Contamination control compromised; product quality and regulatory compliance at risk

personas.name: Quality Engineer
goals: Close CAPAs within target cycle time

personas.name: Plant Manager
dislikedClaims: Metrics that don't reflect plant conditions

personas.name: Warranty Analyst
goals: Predict failure modes before field escalation

formulas.name: Torque Quality Failure Cost
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Cost = (Baseline Torque Reject Rate - Target Rate) x Annual Units x (Rework Cost + Warranty Cost per Unit) + Recall Risk Value
requiredInputs: Baseline torque Cpk or reject rate
Target torque Cpk or reject rate
Warranty claim cost per fastener failure
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: Cpk 0.8 -> 1.67, 500K units, $45 rework, $280 warranty, 2% recall prob x $5M = $2.1M + $0.4M = $2.5M

formulas.name: BOM Revision Error Cost
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Cost = ECOs per Year x (Wrong Part Scrap Cost + Expediting Cost + Engineering Rework Hours x Rate)
requiredInputs: Scrap cost per wrong part incident

confidenceRules.condition: Scrap cost from material and labor standards

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 240 ECOs, 15% wrong part rate, $12K scrap, $8K expedite, 8 hrs @ $150 = $1.15M + $0.48M + $0.29M = $1.92M

formulas.name: Tool Condition Monitoring Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Scrap Reduction from Early Replacement + Avoided CMM Queue + Extended Tool Life Value) - Sensor Investment Amortization
requiredInputs: Baseline scrap rate from tool wear
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 6% -> 2% scrap, $80M material, 200 hrs CMM saved @ $200/hr, 20% tool life x $2M tools = $3.2M + $0.04M + $0.4M - $0.3M = $3.34M

formulas.name: Assembly Line Balance Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Throughput Increase x Margin per Unit x Operating Days) + Overtime Reduction + WIP Carrying Cost Reduction

confidenceRules.condition: Takt time and station times measured

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 78% -> 92% efficiency, 800 units/day, $120 margin, 250 days, 15 -> 5 OT hrs/week, $4M WIP reduction = $3.36M + $0.52M + $0.48M = $4.36M

formulas.name: Welding Quality Improvement Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Weld Reject Reduction x Weld Count x Rework Cost) + NDT Cost Reduction + Avoided Field Failure Cost
requiredInputs: Field failure rate from weld defects

confidenceRules.condition: Weld reject data from WPS records
Field failure root cause linked to welds

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 4% -> 1% reject, 2M welds, $85 rework, $12 NDT, 0.01% field x $50K = $5.1M + $0.72M + $0.1M = $5.92M

formulas.name: SMT Quality Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (DPMO Reduction / 1M x Board Volume x Board Cost) + Rework Labor Reduction + AOI Programming Efficiency Gain
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 5000 -> 800 DPMO, 1M boards, $45 cost, 0.5 hr rework @ $65, 200 -> 80 AOI hrs = $1.89M + $0.14M + $0.05M = $2.08M

formulas.name: CMM Bottleneck Elimination
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Queue Time Reduction x Hourly Production Value) + Inspection Labor Cost Reduction + First Article Cycle Reduction Value
requiredInputs: Current CMM queue hours per week
Hourly production value of downstream operations
New product revenue per month delayed
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 40 -> 10 hrs/week, $8K/hr, $1.2M inspect labor -> $0.9M, 5 -> 2 days FAI, $0.5M/month = $12.5M + $0.3M + $1.5M = $14.3M

formulas.name: Casting Scrap Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Scrap Rate Reduction x Annual Pour Weight x Material Cost per lb) + Rework Reduction + Energy Cost Avoided
requiredInputs: Melting energy cost per unit weight

confidenceRules.condition: Scrap root cause mapped (porosity, shrinkage, etc.)

formulas.name: Kitting Accuracy Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Kitting Error Reduction x Assembly Stops per Error x Downtime Cost) + Rework Avoidance + Inventory Accuracy Improvement
requiredInputs: Average line stop minutes per kitting error

confidenceRules.condition: Kitting errors tracked with station attribution

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 1.5% -> 0.2% errors, 2M kits, 15 min stop @ $200/min, $350 rework = $7.8M + $0.46M + $0.2M = $8.46M

formulas.name: Robot Cycle Time Optimization
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Cycle Time Reduction x Units per Hour x Operating Hours x Margin) + Programming Labor Reduction + Collision Avoidance Value

confidenceRules.condition: Cycle time measured with stopwatch or controller data

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 42s -> 35s cycle, 85 units/hr, 6K hrs, $18 margin, 800 -> 200 prog hrs @ $120, 2 collisions @ $15K = $7.14M + $0.07M + $0.03M = $7.24M

formulas.name: EV Battery Assembly Contamination Cost
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Cost Avoided = (Pack Failure Rate Reduction x Annual Pack Volume x Pack Cost) + Warranty Reserve Reduction + Recall Risk Avoidance
requiredInputs: Baseline battery pack early failure rate %

confidenceRules.condition: Failure root cause linked to contamination

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 0.5% -> 0.1% failure, 200K packs, $12K cost, 2% -> 1% reserve x $2B, 1% recall x $50M = $9.6M + $20M + $0.5M = $30.1M

formulas.name: Nadcap Audit Readiness Cost
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Audit Prep Labor Reduction + Finding Remediation Cost Reduction + Avoided Customer SCAR Cost) + Revenue Protection Value
requiredInputs: Baseline audit prep hours per special process
Revenue at risk from certification loss

confidenceRules.condition: Audit history and finding categories available

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 120 -> 40 hrs x 5 processes @ $150, 8 -> 2 findings x $25K, 4 -> 0 SCARs x $50K = $0.6M + $0.15M + $0.2M = $0.95M + revenue protection

formulas.name: Medical Device V&V Acceleration
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Time-to-Market Reduction x Monthly Revenue per Product x Product Count) + Audit Finding Avoidance + Engineering Efficiency Gain
requiredInputs: Average monthly revenue per new product
Average audit findings from V&V gaps

confidenceRules.condition: V&V process mapped with bottleneck identification

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 180 -> 90 days, $1.5M/month, 3 products, 6 -> 1 findings x $30K, 500 hrs freed @ $150 = $13.5M + $0.15M + $0.08M = $13.73M

formulas.name: Sheet Metal Setup Time Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Setup Time Reduction per Changeover x Changeovers per Week x Weeks x Hourly Value) + First Article Pass Improvement + Scrap Reduction

confidenceRules.condition: Setup time measured with time study

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 45 -> 15 min, 30 changes/week, 50 weeks, $6K/hr, 75% -> 95% FAI, 3% -> 1% scrap = $4.5M + $0.3M + $0.4M = $5.2M

formulas.name: Digital Thread (CAD-to-Machine) Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = CAM Programming Labor Reduction + Scrap Avoidance from Program Errors + First Article Cycle Reduction + Engineering Change Propagation Speed
requiredInputs: Current CAM programming hours per new part
Program error scrap incidents per year
Revenue per day of earlier delivery
ECO volume and propagation delay cost

confidenceRules.condition: CAM process mapped with time study

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 12 -> 3 hrs, 500 parts, $120/hr, 15 errors @ $8K, 2 days saved x $50K/day, 100 ECOs x $2K = $0.54M + $0.12M + $0.1M + $0.2M = $0.96M

benchmarks.name: EV Battery Pack Early Failure - World Class
benchmarks.source: SAE J3234 Battery Assembly Working Group

benchmarks.name: AM Build Success Rate - Metal Powder Bed
Sheet Metal First Article Pass - Automated Brake
V&V Cycle Time - Class II Medical Devices

regulatoryFactors.name: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All discrete manufacturers seeking certification or customer qualification
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Loss of certification → customer exclusion; revenue risk; audit findings

regulatoryFactors.name: IATF 16949:2016 Automotive Quality
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: OEM supplier exclusion; cannot bid new programs; potential line-down penalties

regulatoryFactors.name: AS9100D Aerospace Quality
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Aerospace and defense suppliers including primes and tiers
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Contract ineligibility; Boeing/Airbus supplier status revoked; DCMA rejection

regulatoryFactors.name: FDA 21 CFR Part 820 (QSR) Medical Devices
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Medical device manufacturers in US market
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Warning letter → consent decree → product seizure; $100M+ potential; criminal referral possible

regulatoryFactors.name: FDA Unique Device Identification (UDI)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Medical device manufacturers (Class I, II, III)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Import refusal; product detention; recall requirements

regulatoryFactors.name: Nadcap Special Process Accreditation
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Nadcap (National Aerospace and Defense Contractors Accreditation Program)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Aerospace suppliers performing heat treat, NDT, chemical processing, welding, composites
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Loss of prime supplier status; cannot process flight-critical parts

regulatoryFactors.name: RoHS / REACH Substance Restrictions
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Electronics, electrical equipment, and component manufacturers selling in EU
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Market exclusion; product recall; fines per member state

regulatoryFactors.name: Conflict Minerals (Dodd-Frank 1502 / EU Regulation)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Public companies and EU importers using tin, tungsten, tantalum, gold
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: SEC enforcement; EU customs blocking; customer rejection

regulatoryFactors.name: UL / CSA Product Safety Certification
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Electrical and electronic equipment sold in North America
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Cannot ship to US/Canada; product recall if non-compliant in market

regulatoryFactors.name: CE Marking (EU Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Industrial machinery and robotics sold in EU
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Market exclusion; customs detention; national enforcement fines

regulatoryFactors.name: DO-178C / DO-254 Avionics Software/Hardware
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Cannot obtain FAA/EASA Type Certificate; program cancellation

regulatoryFactors.name: AEC-Q100 / AEC-Q200 Automotive Component Qualification
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Automotive Electronics Council Q100 (ICs) / Q200 (Passive Components)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Semiconductor and passive component suppliers to automotive
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Cannot supply automotive programs; tier-1 supplier exclusion

technologySystems.name: Manufacturing Execution System (MES) - Discrete
technologySystems.description: Shop-floor execution with work order dispatch, operation sequencing, and genealogy tracking for discrete assembly and machining

technologySystems.name: Product Lifecycle Management (PLM)
technologySystems.description: BOM management, engineering change control, CAD integration, and design-to-manufacturing collaboration

technologySystems.name: CAD/CAM / CNC Programming
technologySystems.description: Computer-aided design, manufacturing, and CNC program generation with post-processor management

technologySystems.name: Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) Software
technologySystems.description: Dimensional measurement programming, execution, and statistical analysis for CMMs and optical comparators

technologySystems.name: Statistical Process Control (SPC)
technologySystems.description: Real-time statistical analysis of process variables with control charting, capability analysis, and alarm management

technologySystems.name: Machine Vision / Automated Optical Inspection
technologySystems.description: Automated visual defect detection using cameras, lighting, and AI/ML for assembly verification and dimensional checks

technologySystems.name: Robot Programming / Simulation
technologySystems.description: Offline robot programming, path optimization, collision detection, and cycle time simulation

technologySystems.name: Tool Management System
technologySystems.description: Tool crib management, tool life tracking, presetting integration, and tooling cost analytics

technologySystems.name: Tool Condition Monitoring (TCM)
technologySystems.description: Real-time monitoring of tool wear through spindle load, vibration, acoustic emission, and vision

technologySystems.name: Welding Data Monitoring
technologySystems.description: Real-time weld parameter logging, WPS compliance monitoring, and welder certification tracking

technologySystems.name: Additive Manufacturing Management
technologySystems.description: Build preparation, nesting, parameter management, and production tracking for industrial 3D printing

technologySystems.name: Quality Management System (QMS) - Discrete
technologySystems.description: Document control, CAPA, NCR, audit management, and supplier quality with discrete manufacturing workflows

technologySystems.name: Andon / Production Visualization
technologySystems.description: Real-time production status boards, andon light systems, and operator alert management

technologySystems.name: Electronic Work Instructions (EWI)
technologySystems.description: Digital operator guidance with photos, videos, torque/sequence enforcement, and sign-off capture

technologySystems.name: Digital Twin / Process Simulation
technologySystems.description: Virtual commissioning, process simulation, and production system optimization for discrete lines

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently manage torque tool calibration schedules, and what is your audit pass rate on fastener joints?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess warranty risk from fastening inconsistency and identify tool monitoring gaps

discoveryQuestions.question: When an engineering change is released, how long does it take for the shop floor to see the updated BOM and work instructions?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Map ECO propagation delay and BOM synchronization gaps

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently decide when to change cutting tools—by time, part count, or condition? What is your scrap rate attributed to tool wear?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Identify tool monitoring maturity and scrap root cause quantification

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current line balance efficiency, and how often do you rebalance stations for mixed-model production?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess throughput constraint from line imbalance and dynamic scheduling capability

discoveryQuestions.question: For critical welds, are you monitoring current, voltage, and gas flow in real time, or relying on post-weld inspection?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess welding process control maturity and NDT burden

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current CMM queue time as a percentage of total manufacturing lead time?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify inspection bottleneck and identify metrology capacity constraints

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you manage component traceability—by lot, by serial, or by batch—and can you execute a recall in under 4 hours?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess traceability maturity and recall readiness for regulated segments

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your robot cells are programmed offline versus via teach pendant, and how often are paths re-optimized?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess robotics programming efficiency and continuous improvement practice

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your additive manufacturing build success rate, and what is your ratio of post-processing time to build time?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess AM production readiness and cost structure

discoveryQuestions.question: For sheet metal bending, how many trial bends are typically required before first article approval on a new program?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess press brake setup efficiency and first-article capability

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you validate your cleanroom environment for battery assembly—continuous monitoring, periodic sampling, or event-triggered?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess contamination control rigor and data integrity

discoveryQuestions.question: When was your last Nadcap audit, how many findings were there, and what was the root cause category distribution?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess special process control maturity and prime supplier risk

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your average V&V cycle time from protocol approval to final report, and how much is spent on manual traceability matrix maintenance?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify validation bottleneck and automation opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently manage semiconductor allocation alerts, and what is your annual expediting spend on allocated components?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify supply constraint impact and component lifecycle management maturity

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you ensure the CNC program version running on the machine matches the current CAD revision in your PLM?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess digital thread integrity and version control gaps

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current SMT DPMO, and what percentage of your AOI findings are false positives requiring manual review?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify electronics assembly quality and inspection efficiency

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently manage weld parameter settings—fixed recipes per WPS, or real-time adaptive control based on joint conditions?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess welding process control sophistication and adaptive technology readiness

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your warranty cost per unit, and which failure mode categories are trending upward in the last 12 months?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify warranty exposure and identify field failure patterns

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you manage firmware flashing and software versioning on your electronic assemblies—is it automated with verification?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess firmware/software configuration control in production

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your customer SCAR rate per million parts shipped, and which customers are the most active in supplier quality programs?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify customer quality pressure and identify improvement priorities

objections.objection: We already have MES and ERP connected—why would we need another layer?
objections.underlyingConcern: Integration fatigue and skepticism about incremental value from additional software
objections.reframe: This is not a replacement layer; it augments the MES-to-ERP gap with vertical intelligence. Example: your MES knows a part was made but doesn't correlate CMM drift with tool wear index in real time. The value is in cross-system signal correlation, not system replacement.

objections.objection: Our customers (OEMs/primes) dictate our systems—we can't add unapproved platforms.
objections.underlyingConcern: Fear of customer audit non-compliance and losing supplier status
objections.reframe: The platform integrates with your existing validated QMS/MES and enhances audit readiness. For IATF/AS9100, it actually strengthens evidence trails and CAPA tracking—customers want data-driven quality, not more paper.

objections.objection: We don't have the IT infrastructure for real-time machine monitoring.
objections.underlyingConcern: Capital constraint for sensors, networking, and edge computing
objections.reframe: Start with data you already have: CMM logs, torque controller records, weld parameter files. Many discrete manufacturers achieve $500K+ value from existing data before adding a single new sensor. Phased approach preserves capital.

objections.objection: Our workforce is aging and resistant to new technology.
objections.underlyingConcern: Change management risk and operator acceptance
objections.reframe: Solutions are designed for the operator, not the data scientist. Example: electronic work instructions with photo/video guidance reduce training time by 40% and help retire knowledge. The floor sees immediate benefit, not burden.

objections.objection: We've tried predictive maintenance before and didn't see ROI.
objections.underlyingConcern: Previous vendor failure and skepticism about AI/ML claims
objections.reframe: Predictive maintenance ROI depends on asset criticality and data quality. In discrete manufacturing, the highest ROI often comes from targeted applications: tool wear prediction on high-value CNC assets, not fleet-wide vibration monitoring. We scope to high-impact, data-rich use cases first.

objections.objection: Our quality system is already validated—we can't change anything without re-validation.
objections.underlyingConcern: Regulatory re-validation cost and timeline, especially in medical/aerospace
objections.reframe: The platform operates as a reporting and analytics overlay on existing validated systems (ERP, MES, QMS). It does not replace or modify the validated process. For FDA/AS9100, it strengthens the quality system evidence without altering the controlled process.

objections.objection: We make too many different parts in small volumes—data analytics won't work for us.
objections.underlyingConcern: High-mix, low-volume (HMLV) manufacturing skepticism about pattern detection
objections.reframe: HMLV environments benefit from family-based analytics: grouping by process family, material, or feature type. A 50-part family with 200 annual runs generates statistically meaningful patterns. The segmentation logic is built for this.

objections.objection: Our CAM programmers are experts—we don't need automated programming.
objections.underlyingConcern: Job security and underestimation of programming bottleneck impact
objections.reframe: The value isn't replacing expertise; it's eliminating repetitive work. Typical CAM programmers spend 60% of time on routine geometry and 40% on complex optimization. Automation handles the routine, freeing experts for high-value optimization and NPI acceleration.

objections.objection: EV battery assembly is too new—there are no proven benchmarks.
objections.underlyingConcern: Fear of being early adopter in nascent technology domain
objections.reframe: While EV battery pack assembly is evolving, contamination control, welding monitoring, and torque management are mature disciplines with proven ROI. The fundamentals transfer directly. Early movers are establishing the benchmarks that will define the industry.

objections.objection: We don't have the data science talent to interpret these signals.
objections.underlyingConcern: Skills gap and dependency on vendor for ongoing value extraction
objections.reframe: The platform delivers prescriptive, not raw, insights. Example: instead of 'spindle load anomaly detected,' you receive 'Tool T-47 on Mill M3 predicted to exceed tolerance in 12 parts—recommend change after Job 2847.' No data science required.

buyingTriggers.trigger: Major OEM issues supplier quality mandate requiring real-time process data sharing
Warranty claim spike triggers executive-level quality review and root cause mandate
New product introduction (NPI) timeline compressed by customer; existing V&V process too slow
Nadcap or IATF audit findings exceed threshold; certification at risk
CMM backlog causing daily production delays and overtime for inspection staff
New EV battery assembly line investment; seeking contamination control and traceability systems
Customer SCAR volume triggers executive action plan with 90-day improvement mandate
Robot collision or near-miss triggers safety review and path optimization initiative
FDA 483 or warning letter issued; CAPA effectiveness questioned
New aerospace contract requires AS9100 and Nadcap with real-time special process data
Semiconductor allocation notice forces production mix changes and expediting crisis
Plant capacity expansion planned; seeking to avoid proportional inspection headcount growth
Additive manufacturing qualification achieved; now scaling to production volumes and hitting yield walls
Digital transformation initiative launched by corporate; plant manager must select and implement use cases
Sheet metal or fabrication department losing bids due to long quoting and setup times

workedExamples.title: Torque Cpk Decline → Warranty Risk → Assembly Process Control Investment
workedExamples.scenario: Tier-1 automotive supplier producing EV battery tray assemblies observes torque Cpk declining from 1.67 to 1.2 over 3 months at Station 14 (battery module fastening).

signalsDetected.signal: Warranty claims increasing for battery connection issues
Tool calibration overdue on 40% of fastening tools

workedExamples.hypothesis: Battery module fastening torque inconsistency is the leading predictor of early-life battery pack failures. The root cause is calibration drift and lack of statistical process control on DC electric tools.
calculationWalkthrough: Step 1: Baseline Cpk = 1.2, Target = 1.67. Reject rate at Cpk 1.2 = 0.32% (Z=2.4, 2-tail). At Cpk 1.67 = 0.006%.
Step 2: Annual volume = 450,000 battery trays. Excess reject rate = 0.314%.
Step 3: Excess defects = 1,413 units/year.
Step 4: Rework cost per defect = $65 (disassembly, re-torque, re-test). Rework cost = $91,845.
Step 5: Warranty cost per field failure traced to fastener = $850 (truck roll, part, labor). Historical traceability shows 18% of defects escape to field. Warranty cost = 254 x $850 = $215,900.

Step 6: Recall risk: OEM recall threshold is 0.1% failure rate. Current trajectory 0.06%, trending toward threshold. Estimated recall cost if crossed = $2.5M (small recall scenario). Risk-adjusted (15% probability) = $375,000.
Step 7: Total annual cost of quality gap = $91,845 + $215,900 + $375,000 = $682,745.
Step 8: Investment in tool condition monitoring + SPC station upgrade = $180,000. Payback = 3.2 months. 3-year NPV at 12% discount = $1.52M.
customerValidationRequired: Validate warranty claim root cause distribution (is 18% fastener-related?)
Confirm torque tool calibration interval and drift data
Confirm OEM recall threshold and escalation protocol

workedExamples.title: CMM Queue Growth → Throughput Constraint → In-Process Metrology Investment
workedExamples.scenario: Precision aerospace machine shop producing titanium structural brackets experiences CMM queue growing from 8 hours to 35 hours per week. First article inspection cycle extends from 2 days to 6 days. Customer (Boeing) delivery performance drops below 95%.

signalsDetected.signal: CMM queue hours trending up >300% over 6 months
First article inspection cycle time >5 days
Customer on-time delivery <95% with inspection as bottleneck
Inspection staff overtime >25% and headcount freeze in place

workedExamples.hypothesis: CMM capacity is the primary bottleneck constraining throughput and delivery performance. The highest-ROI intervention is deploying in-process metrology (on-machine probing + optical scanning) for feature classes that don't require CMM accuracy.
calculationWalkthrough: Step 1: Current queue = 35 hrs/week, target = 10 hrs/week. Queue reduction = 25 hrs/week.
Step 2: Hourly production value constrained by inspection = $6,800 (titanium parts, high value). Queue time value = 25 x 50 weeks x $6,800 = $8.5M.
Step 3: However, demand constraint applies: shop is at 85% capacity, not 100%. Effective throughput gain = 15% of queue value = $1.28M.
Step 4: Inspection labor cost: 3 FTE x $85K + 25% OT premium = $319K. Target: 2.5 FTE + 10% OT = $234K. Savings = $85K.
Step 5: First article cycle reduction: 6 -> 3 days. New product value at risk = $200K/month revenue x 3 months delay risk. Cycle reduction probability-weighted value = $180K.
Step 6: On-machine probing investment (Renishaw OMP60 + software) = $45K per machine x 5 machines = $225K. Optical scanning station = $120K. Total = $345K.
Step 7: Annual value = $1.28M + $85K + $180K = $1.545M. Payback = 2.7 months. 3-year NPV = $3.52M.

businessOutcome.riskReduction: Customer delivery scorecard recovery to >98%, contract renewal secured
businessOutcome.cashFlowImprovement: WIP velocity increase reduces inventory carrying by $420K

customerValidationRequired: Confirm CMM queue attribution (is inspection truly the bottleneck, not machining?)
Validate on-machine probing accuracy against customer GD&T requirements
Confirm demand exists to absorb 15% throughput increase

workedExamples.title: SMT DPMO Spike → Electronics Rework Crisis → Process Control Enhancement
workedExamples.scenario: Medical device electronics contract manufacturer experiences SMT DPMO rising from 800 to 4,200 over 4 months. AOI false positive rate grows to 18%, flooding the rework station. FDA pre-approval inspection scheduled in 6 months.

signalsDetected.signal: SMT DPMO trending up 5x over 4 months

signalsDetected.dataSource: AOI system logs + manual verification records

signalsDetected.signal: Reflow oven temperature zone 3 showing 3°C drift from recipe
Solder paste volume deposition CV increasing from 8% to 14%

workedExamples.hypothesis: Reflow profile drift combined with solder paste volume inconsistency is causing solder joint defects (voids, bridging). The AOI system is miscalibrated for the new defect signature, creating false positives. The combination threatens FDA readiness and product reliability.
calculationWalkthrough: Step 1: Baseline DPMO = 4,200, target = 800. Excess DPMO = 3,400.
Step 2: Annual board volume = 850,000. Excess defects = 2,890.
Step 3: Board material + placement cost = $78. Excess material cost = 2,890 x $78 = $225,420.
Step 4: Rework labor: 2,890 defects x 0.4 hours x $72/hr = $83,232.
Step 5: AOI false positive labor: 18% FP rate on 850K boards = 153K false calls. At 2 min/call x $72/hr = $367,200.
Step 6: FDA risk: 483 observation on process control could delay product launch by 6 months. Revenue at risk = $3.5M/month x 6 months x 30% probability = $6.3M risk-adjusted.
Step 7: Reflow oven controller upgrade + KIC profiler integration = $85K. AOI recipe optimization + SPI closed-loop = $45K. Total = $130K.
Step 8: Annual direct savings = $225K + $83K + $367K = $675K. Payback = 2.3 months. With FDA risk: value = $6.97M. 3-year NPV = $1.73M (direct only) or $15.2M (with FDA risk).

businessOutcome.riskReduction: FDA 483 avoidance (30% → <5% probability); product launch protection
businessOutcome.cashFlowImprovement: Reduced WIP in rework queue = $120K; expedited delivery penalties avoided

customerValidationRequired: Validate reflow profile drift correlation with specific defect types
Confirm AOI false positive rate measurement methodology
Assess FDA inspection scope and process control emphasis areas

competitorFactors.typicalPositioning: Full digital twin and Industry 4.0 platform

valueDrivers.name: Warranty Cost Reduction - Discrete
valueDrivers.description: Reduction in warranty claims, field failures, and recall risk through upstream process control in assembly, fastening, welding, and electronics.
valueDrivers.formula: Annual Savings = (Baseline Failure Rate - Target Failure Rate) x Annual Units x Warranty Cost per Unit + Recall Risk Value

valueDrivers.name: Scrap Reduction - Machining & Fabrication
valueDrivers.description: Reduction in material and labor scrap from tool wear prediction, first article improvement, and process parameter control.
valueDrivers.formula: Annual Savings = (Scrap Rate Reduction) x Annual Material Cost + Rework Reduction + Energy Avoidance

valueDrivers.name: Inspection Bottleneck Elimination
valueDrivers.description: Throughput improvement from reducing CMM queue, automating in-process metrology, and accelerating first article inspection.
valueDrivers.formula: Annual Value = Queue Time Reduction x Hourly Production Value + Labor Reduction + FAI Acceleration Revenue

valueDrivers.name: Assembly Throughput Improvement
valueDrivers.description: Output increase from line balancing, kitting accuracy, sequence compliance, and robot optimization.
valueDrivers.formula: Annual Value = Throughput Increase x Margin per Unit x Operating Days + Overtime Reduction

valueDrivers.name: Regulatory Risk Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Avoidance of audit findings, warning letters, certification loss, and customer SCARs through proactive compliance management.
valueDrivers.formula: Annual Value = Audit Finding Cost Reduction + Customer SCAR Avoidance + Certification Loss Revenue Protection

valueDrivers.name: Engineering Change Efficiency
valueDrivers.description: Cost and time reduction from accelerated ECO propagation, BOM synchronization, and digital thread integrity.
valueDrivers.formula: Annual Value = ECO Cycle Reduction x Engineering Hour Value + Wrong-Part Scrap Avoidance + Expediting Reduction

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Extended from generic scrap rate to discrete-specific scrap categories: casting scrap, welding reject, SMT DPMO, sheet metal first article, AM build failure. Master scrap rate remains as aggregate.
Enhanced with discrete manufacturing specific pressures (NPI deadline compression, mixed-model complexity, customer SCAR volume) and trusted evidence specific to plant-level discrete KPIs.
Extended with discrete-specific yield categories: SMT first pass, sheet metal first article, welding first pass, battery pack assembly yield.
Replaced with more granular discrete signal: CNC spindle load anomaly (tool wear) and robot collision log growth. Machine downtime remains in master as generic rule.
